mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that outweighs monetary support, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 58% of contraction periods during tightening cycles, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 0.1% signals disinflationary pressure that forces equity models to lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of disinflationary months, or the threshold condition yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth (Spearman rho < 0.05), or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, indicates weakening consumer demand that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term between GDP and retail sales is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates robust capacity utilization and labor absorption, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of recovery periods with these supply signals, or the mechanism fails to predict direction when volatility indices exceed 25.
